product_overview: Huginn is a media-intelligence service that accepts a news-article URL and returns a 3-layer report: ownership chain of the outlet, author history/patterns, and an AI-generated contrast analysis combining both layers. The pipeline uses Postlight Parser, SerpApi, Wikidata SPARQL, DuckDuckGo Instant Answer, and OpenRouter LLM with graceful degradation when external services fail.
core_goals: Provide a concise 3-layer media intelligence report for any news article URL: ownership chain, author history, and AI contrast analysis.
Maintain graceful degradation: if an external API fails or keys are missing, return partial results without breaking the pipeline.
Keep the stack minimal and privacy-aware; reuse DuckDuckGo Instant Answer where possible and avoid unnecessary dependencies.
Expose a single backend endpoint POST /api/analyze that orchestrates all data sources and returns structured JSON suitable for UI rendering.

features.name: Media Analysis API
features.description: Server-side pipeline that ingests a news article URL and returns a structured analysis including article metadata, ownership chain from Wikidata, author history (SerpApi + DuckDuckGo), and an LLM-generated contrast analysis (OpenRouter). The endpoint performs parallel searches and aggregates results, degrading gracefully when services are unavailable or API keys are missing.
user_flows: POST /api/analyze with body { "url": "https://example.com/news/article" } -> Invoke @postlight/parser.parse(url) on server to extract title, author, domain, content (first 500 chars) -> GET https://serpapi.com/search?engine=google_news&q=<encoded title>&api_key=SERPAPI_KEY to collect globalContext (20 results) -> GET https://serpapi.com/search?engine=google_news&q=<encoded author+medium>&api_key=SERPAPI_KEY to collect authorContext (10 results) -> GET https://query.wikidata.org/sparql?query=<sparql-for-domain> (Accept: application/sparql-results+json) to retrieve ownership chain -> GET https://api.duckduckgo.com/?q=<encoded author>&format=json to enrich author bio and articles as fallback/additional context -> POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 with OPENROUTER_API_KEY and payload containing title, author, domain, ownership chain, globalContext snippets, authorContext snippets to produce JSON {ownershipSummary, authorPattern, contrastAnalysis} -> Return 200 with body { article, ownership, authorHistory, contrast, raw }
POST /api/analyze with missing or empty body -> Server validates input and returns 400 with { "error": "url is required" }

features.name: Graceful Degradation & Fallbacks
features.description: Pipeline behavior when external services are unavailable or API keys are missing: continue processing with available data, populate fields with explicit fallback markers (e.g., 'No encontrado en Wikidata', 'No disponible'), and log intermediate results for debugging.
user_flows: POST /api/analyze with body { "url": "https://example.com/news/article" } -> Invoke @postlight/parser.parse(url) and receive parsed article metadata -> GET https://serpapi.com/search?engine=google_news&q=<title>&api_key=SERPAPI_KEY -> Receive 401 Unauthorized when SERPAPI_KEY is not set -> GET https://api.duckduckgo.com/?q=<author>&format=json to obtain authorContext fallback -> GET https://query.wikidata.org/sparql?query=<sparql-for-domain> -> Receive empty results and return ownership chain as [ ] or { owner: "No encontrado en Wikidata" } -> POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 without OPENROUTER_API_KEY (or model fails) -> Receive fallback string responses 'No disponible' from openrouter wrapper -> Return 200 with partial analysis JSON where missing pieces are explicitly labeled and raw arrays contain whatever context was fetched

POST /api/analyze with URL from a client-side rendered site where @postlight/parser fails to extract author/title -> @postlight/parser.parse(url) returns partial/empty fields -> Server falls back to reading OG meta tags from page fetch or returns title/author as empty strings -> Continue subsequent calls using available values -> Return 200 with article fields possibly populated from OG meta and note in logs that parser fallback occurred

features.name: Web UI (Input + Raw JSON Output)
features.description: Simple frontend page that allows the user to paste a news article URL and submit it. For now the page displays the raw JSON response returned by the backend /api/analyze endpoint.
user_flows: GET / -> Server renders page with URL input and 'Analyze' button -> Client POST /api/analyze with body { "url": "https://example.com/news/article" } -> Receive 200 and display raw JSON response on the page
GET / -> Server renders page -> Client POST /api/analyze with body { } (missing url) -> Receive 400 { "error": "url is required" } and display error to user

tech_stack.other: Postlight Parser, SerpApi, Wikidata SPARQL, DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API, OpenRouter LLM

features.name: Media Analysis API
features.description: Analyzes a news article URL and returns a 3-layer media intelligence report covering ownership chain, author history, and AI-generated contrast analysis

endpoints.description: Receives a news article URL and returns ownership chain (Wikidata), author history (SerpApi + DuckDuckGo), and contrast analysis (OpenRouter LLM)

known_limitations.issue: Postlight Parser fails to extract title/author from sites using client-side rendering (e.g. CNN); OG meta fallback is used
known_limitations.impact: Author and title may fall back to OG meta tags instead of article body

known_limitations.issue: Wikidata SPARQL may not find lesser-known media outlets
known_limitations.impact: Ownership chain returns empty with "No encontrado en Wikidata"

known_limitations.issue: SerpApi returns 401 when SERPAPI_KEY is not set
known_limitations.impact: authorContext and globalContext will be empty arrays; DuckDuckGo still runs as fallback

known_limitations.issue: OpenRouter returns fallback strings when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is not set or model fails
known_limitations.impact: contrast.analysis, authorHistory.pattern, and ownership.summary from LLM will show "No disponible"

known_limitations.issue: DuckDuckGo RelatedTopics may return sparse results for authors without Wikipedia pages
known_limitations.impact: authorHistory.bio will be empty string and articles list may be short
